source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the Cartesian coordinate system, the ray OA in the first quadrant forms an angle $\alpha$ with the positive x-axis. If $\sin\alpha =\frac{3}{5}$, what could be the coordinates of point P located on ray OA?
source_intake_record.raw_answer_text:
\textbf{Solution:} Draw PB $\perp$ OB at point B through point P.\\
A, $\because$ point P (3, 5), $\therefore$ OB = 3, PB = 5,\\
$\therefore$ OP = $\sqrt{34}$ (by the Pythagorean theorem),\\
$\therefore \sin\alpha = \frac{PB}{OP} = \frac{5}{\sqrt{34}} = \frac{5\sqrt{34}}{34} \ne \frac{3}{5}$, thus this option does not meet the criteria;\\
B, $\because$ point P (5, 3), $\therefore$ OB = 5, PB = 3,\\
$\therefore$ OP = $\sqrt{34}$ (by the Pythagorean theorem),\\
$\therefore \sin\alpha = \frac{PB}{OP} = \frac{3}{\sqrt{34}} = \frac{3\sqrt{34}}{34} \ne \frac{3}{5}$, thus this option does not meet the criteria;\\
C, $\because$ point P (4, 3), $\therefore$ OB = 4, PB = 3,\\
$\therefore$ OP = $5$ (by the Pythagorean theorem),\\
$\therefore \sin\alpha = \frac{PB}{OP} = \frac{3}{5}$, thus this option meets the criteria;\\
D, $\because$ point P (3, 4), $\therefore$ OB = 3, PB = 4,\\
$\therefore$ OP = $5$ (by the Pythagorean theorem),\\
$\therefore \sin\alpha = \frac{PB}{OP} = \frac{4}{5} \ne \frac{3}{5}$, thus this option does not meet the criteria;\\
Therefore, the answer is: \boxed{C}.

raw_answer_text mapped from boxed option C to its corresponding coordinate text

choices are explicit and finite, making correctness easy to validate automatically
